blocks.heading: A deterministic hockey-league life-sim: generate a world, simulate seasons, let role-based AI run the franchises, and narrate what happens.

rows.value: A single-player sports RPG where you watch a whole NHL-shaped league live: games, careers, trades, firings, and storylines, all simulated.
Domain logic in `lib/` (7 domains); a Next.js `app/` reads it through an in-memory store. No database — state lives in memory for the session.
Everything hangs off one loop: `generateLeague` → `simSeason` → (per day) brains decide & the story engine narrates → offseason → repeat.

primer.h: No god-mode AI — four roles each perceive their own (wrong) world.
primer.p: GMs, coaches, owners, and players each evaluate the league through noisy, skill-scaled perception. A weak GM literally *mis-sees* talent. This is the heart of `lib/brain`.

primer.h: Determinism is a hard contract — same seed, same league, same season.
primer.p: Every random draw comes from a seeded RNG (`createRng`); nothing calls `Math.random()`. This is what makes the calibration harness and replays possible.

primer.h: One season loop is the spine the whole codebase hangs off.
primer.p: If you read one function, read `simSeason`: it walks the schedule, calls the game engine, fires the brains between games, and ticks the story engine. Section 2 traces it.

primer.h: State is ephemeral and lives in one Zustand store.
primer.p: There is no persistence layer yet — the generated league lives in `lib/store` for the session and the UI subscribes to it. Reference data (calibration baselines) is the only thing on disk.

blocks.title: The domain map
blocks.intro: The seven domains in four architectural layers. Higher layers depend on the ones below; the engine sits on the data model, intelligence and narrative sit on the engine. The test harness exercises the whole stack.

blocks.caption: Solid arrow = **depends on**; dashed = **exercises**. Higher layers sit on the layers below.

blocks.title: Spine
blocks.intro: The one runtime flow worth memorizing. `store.simSeason()` kicks it off; the gold steps repeat for every game and every day across the 82-game season — that inner loop is where the brains and the story engine do their work — then the offseason runs and the whole thing repeats for the next season.

diagram.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  gen["generateLeague(seed)"] --> season["simSeason()"]
  season -->|per game| game["simGame()"]
  game --> day["runDayTriggers()"]
  day --> story["storyTick.dailyTick()"]
  story -->|next day| season
  season -->|season ends| off["offseason: draft, FA, retire"]
  off -->|next season| season
  classDef changed fill:#ffd43b,stroke:#f08c00,color:#1b1b1b,stroke-width:2px;
  class game,day,story changed;
diagram.caption: Brains never mutate the world directly — they return decisions that `sim` executes. The story engine only observes. That one-way rule keeps the loop deterministic.

blocks.title: The 7 domains
blocks.intro: Each domain owns one slice of the simulation. Three have full deep-dive pages; the rest are mapped here and get pages on demand.

tiles.name: sim
tiles.purpose: The deterministic, low-allocation game engine and the multi-season loop. Resolves hockey shift-by-shift at <2 ms/game and orchestrates the whole season.

tiles.name: brain
tiles.purpose: Six role-based AI brains — GM, Coach, Owner, Player, Scout, Agent — each with its own skill-scaled, noisy perception. No god-mode; weak decision-makers genuinely mis-see the league.

tiles.name: story
tiles.purpose: Turns discrete simulation events into a living narrative: multi-beat story arcs, two-tier memory, news feeds, on-demand prose, and evolving franchise identity.

tiles.name: world
tiles.purpose: The institutional data model and procedural generator: franchises, teams, players, contracts, schedule, plus the identity/reputation system. The schema everything else reads.

tiles.name: profiles
tiles.purpose: Per-entity attributes: player skills, condition, and development; coach, GM, owner, and scout personality and competencies. The numbers the brains perceive and the engine resolves.

tiles.name: store
tiles.purpose: The in-memory Zustand league state. Its `simSeason()` action orchestrates generate → sim → standings, and the `app/` routes subscribe to it. The boundary between the simulation and the UI.

tiles.name: calibration
tiles.purpose: The multi-season test harness: runs N seasons from a fixed seed, collects 200+ metrics (talent spread, PP/PK, comeback rates, re-injury…) and compares them against reference baselines.
